name: Jimmie Lunceford
abstract: James Melvin Lunceford (June 6, 1902 – July 12, 1947) was an American jazz alto saxophonist and bandleader in the swing era.

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: I left Earl and I went to New York to join Jimmy Lunceford... I got an opportunity to play a lot of solos. He had two good trombone players but they weren't really soloists... So, I got -- It was a good break for me going with him because I got most all of the solos to do.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: My brother had introduced me to the sounds of Duke Ellington, Count Basie, and Jimmie Lunceford. The three alto saxophonists who were principal players in these bands became my idols at the time: from the Lunceford orchestra, Willie Smith...
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slam Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: I was able to listen to especially Duke Ellington and -- there were quite a few -- Jimmie Lunceford. And it sort of was -- I was very interested -- the sound really knocked me out, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Radke
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates a story about Corky Corcoran and Jimmie Lunceford: 'Corky told me this story by the way... He calls up Jimmie Lunceford... Mr. Lunceford.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Radke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Hillsdale, Michigan, November 8, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Norris Turney
about_quotes.evidence: And we used to listen to big bands, Fletcher Henderson, Earl Hines, Basie when he first went to New York, and all the big bands you know, Jimmie Lunceford.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Alley
about_quotes.evidence: my favorite band of all time in the beginning was Jimmie Lunceford. Jimmie Lunceford knocked me out. That’s what I tried to pattern my style after.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Alley interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002
